commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves existing non-empty session `label` and `displayName` fields when `upsertSessionEntry` receives replacement metadata with omitted, null, or empty title fields.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +100. Total +124 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main persists a cloned replacement entry in `upsertSessionEntry`, so an existing titled row loses `label` and `displayName` when the replacement omits or empties them.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide whether the exported `upsertSessionEntry` semantics may change as proposed or need explicit documented clear/rename guidance first.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve titles on upsert
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: replacement `upsertSessionEntry` writes can erase stable human-facing session titles when runtime metadata omits or empties `label` / `displayName`.
- Solution: preserve existing non-empty human title fields during replacement upserts unless the incoming entry provides non-empty replacements.
- What changed: session-store upsert merge guard plus focused regression coverage for the default main lane and empty/null replacement metadata.
- Replacement: supersedes #89005 with the same fix rebased onto current `main` and a regression case matching the observed `agent:main:main` webchat/default-lane title wipe.

## Motivation

Persistent sessions can fall back to default/origin-derived names after a runtime replacement write drops stored `label` and `displayName`. The issue is visible as a renamed session even though the transcript still exists. The store boundary is the narrowest durable fix because callers should not need to manually preserve stable human titles when they are only refreshing runtime metadata.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89003
- Replaces #89005
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: an existing `agent:main:main` session with `label` / `displayName` set to a stable human name should not lose those fields when a replacement upsert refreshes runtime metadata from a webchat/default-lane entry that omits titles.
- Real environment observed: local installed OpenClaw runtime on macOS showed `agent:main:main` persisted with `label: null` and `displayName: null`, causing the session picker/status surface to fall back to the default lane name.
- Local repair proof: after restoring the local registry through the existing operational guard, the live session list again reported `label: "cloud-codex-main"` and `displayName: "cloud-codex-main"` for `agent:main:main`.
- Source proof: the new regression test reproduces the same shape: existing default main session with webchat origin and stable title, followed by replacement upsert with webchat origin and omitted title fields.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `upsertSessionEntry` cloned and persisted the replacement entry directly. It preserved legacy ACP cleanup semantics but did not merge existing human title fields.
- Missing guardrail: tests covered replacement behavior and legacy ACP cleanup, but not preserving `label` / `displayName` when runtime replacement metadata omits or empties those fields.
- Why this PR is still needed after #89005: #89005 was left open and unmerged; the current local symptom reappeared. This replacement PR is based on current `main` and adds a default-main-lane test that mirrors the observed session-key/origin shape.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test file: `src/config/sessions.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - default `agent:main:main` webchat replacement upsert preserves `cloud-codex-main` title fields when incoming metadata omits them
  - empty/null title metadata preserves existing non-empty titles
  - explicit non-empty replacement titles still replace previous values

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Persistent session titles remain stable across runtime replacement upserts that do not provide a non-empty new title. Explicit title replacement remains possible by sending non-empty `label` / `displayName` values.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Verification

- `npm test -- src/config/sessions.test.ts` - 52 passed
- `npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/sessions/store.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts`
- `npx oxlint src/config/sessions/store.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Known local validation note:

- `npm run lint:core -- src/config/sessions/store.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts` currently fails before reaching this patch on unrelated plugin SDK declaration checks: missing declarations for `proper-lockfile`, `cross-spawn`, `hosted-git-info`, `rastermill`, plus existing strictness errors in `src/agents/sessions/*`, `src/agents/utils/*`, and `src/media/image-ops.ts`. Direct `oxlint` on the changed files passes.

## Human Verification

I personally verified the focused session-store regression tests, formatting, direct oxlint on touched files, and whitespace checks. I also verified the affected local runtime registry after repair reports the default main lane as `cloud-codex-main` again.
target.author: serkonyc
